subject: Discrete Random Variables
topic: ¬ß16 Expected Value of a Function of a Discrete Random Variable
question: Let $X$ be a discrete random variable. Show that $E(aX^2 + bX + c) = aE(X^2) + bE(X) + c$.
answer: Let D denote the range of X. Then E(aX2 +bX +c) = ‚ąĎ x‚ąąD (ax2 +bx +c)p(x) = ‚ąĎ x‚ąąD ax2p(x) + ‚ąĎ x‚ąąD bxp(x) + ‚ąĎ x‚ąąD cp(x) =a ‚ąĎ x‚ąąD x2p(x) +b ‚ąĎ x‚ąąD xp(x) +c ‚ąĎ x‚ąąD p(x) =aE(X2) +bE(X) +c
explanation: Answer: Let $D$ denote the range of $X$. Then $E(aX^2 + bX + c) = \sum_{x \in D} (ax^2 + bx + c) p(x) = \sum_{x \in D} ax^2 p(x) + \sum_{x \in D} bx p(x) + \sum_{x \in D} c p(x) = a \sum_{x \in D} x^2 p(x) + b \sum_{x \in D} x p(x) + c \sum_{x \in D} p(x) = aE(X^2) + bE(X) + c$
